protocol.name: status-sync
protocol.purpose: Synchronize story status (and optionally sprint_id) between story file (source of truth) and sprint-status.yaml (derived view). Called by any workflow that changes story status. Ensures both files stay in sync at every transition.
protocol.input: story_key, new_status, source_workflow, sprint_id (optional — if provided, update sprint_id field in story file; use "null" to clear it when removing a story from a sprint)

step.title: Update Story File
step.action: Update YAML frontmatter status field to {new_status}
Update YAML frontmatter sprint_id field to {sprint_id}. If sprint_id is "null", set sprint_id to null (clearing the sprint assignment).

Scan the story body (below the frontmatter) for a line matching the pattern "**Status:**" (e.g., "> **Status:** backlog"). If found, update the status value on that line to match {new_status}. This prevents frontmatter/body drift.
This is the source of truth — always updated first

step.title: Update sprint-status.yaml
step.action: Skip — sprint not yet created. No entry to update.
Re-read sprint-status.yaml immediately before writing (write safety per sprint-status-write-safety.xml)
Check if {story_key} has an entry in sprint-status.yaml

action.if: sprint_id is explicitly set to null AND story_key has entry in sprint-status.yaml

step.action: Remove the {story_key} entry from sprint-status.yaml — story is no longer in this sprint.

action.if: sprint_id is NOT null (or not provided) AND story_key has entry

step.action: Update ONLY the status field of the {story_key} entry to {new_status}. Minimal write — do not modify other stories or fields.
Skip — story not yet assigned to a sprint. No entry to update.

If sprint-status.yaml update fails for any reason: log warning "{source_workflow}: Failed to sync sprint-status.yaml for {story_key} → {new_status}. Run /gaia-sprint-status to reconcile." and continue — this is non-blocking.
